title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Runtime Lens: Render Optimization
summary: Optimization starts from intuition, solver and drawing time are combined, huge mazes freeze the event loop, and average latency hides stalls. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the render optimization increment in representative performance and responsiveness report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness.
Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 5543 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness. A defensible solution applies Render Optimization, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to representative performance and responsiveness report.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_performance_profiling_2(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Render Optimization evidence · Theory Render Optimization
steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-render-optimization-slnhpw
# Competency: maze-render-optimization.
# Case 4417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_render_optimization_slnhpw(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_slnhpw = "slnhpw-wphnls-4417"
    assert evidence_variant_slnhpw
    evidence_axes_slnhpw = (
        "gridslnhpw",
        "topology4417",
        "searchwphnls",
        "eventslnhpw4417",
        "access4417slnhpw",
        "recoverywphnls4417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_slnhpw)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Optimization model · Theory Render Optimization, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Render Optimization precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Optimization model · Theory Render Optimization, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4829 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness.
